meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier. Learn to master API integrations using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led guide for student developers.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you ship a functional API-driven application. By connecting real-world data to a clean UI using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical fluency, architectural awareness, and the ability to solve production-grade problems. Don't just learn code—build the bridge between data and user utility.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the syntax, you have the passion, but you lack the proof. In a market flooded with generic tutorials, your challenge isn't learning to code—it's learning to integrate. The difference between a student project and a professional asset is the ability to fetch, parse, and display live data.

We are going to move beyond local JSON files and static mockups. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you will transform a theoretical exercise into a live, API-powered application. This is your first step toward building a portfolio that commands respect from senior engineers.

steps.title: Define Your Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the API response. You need to know exactly what fields you are consuming to prevent runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a weather API response containing current temperature, humidity, and forecast array with daily data points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.ts --content 'define interfaces based on weather api documentation'

steps.title: Establish Secure Connectivity
steps.description: Never hardcode API keys. Use environment variables and a secure fetch wrapper to handle authentication and error states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure API fetch utility using Axios that handles 401, 403, and 500 error states with typed responses.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature --path src/lib/api.ts --specs 'implement secure axios wrapper with error handling'

steps.title: Implement Data Transformation
steps.description: Raw API data is rarely UI-ready. Use a transformation layer to sanitize and format data before it hits your components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that transforms raw API weather data into a simplified UI model for a dashboard component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file src/utils/transform.ts --transform 'map raw api response to component-ready state'

steps.title: Build the UI Bridge
steps.description: Connect your processed data to the frontend. Focus on loading states and empty states to show professional attention to detail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React dashboard component that displays the transformed weather data with loading skeletons and error alerts.

steps.title: End-to-End Integration Test
steps.description: Verify the flow from API request to DOM render. A working app is your proof of concept.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script that mocks the API response and verifies the dashboard displays the correct temperature.

pro_tips: Always implement a 'caching layer' with React Query or SWR to prevent excessive API calls and improve perceived performance.
Use Zod for runtime schema validation; it catches breaking API changes before they crash your production UI.
Add a 'debug mode' toggle in your UI that displays raw API responses—hiring managers love seeing how you debug complex data.
Document your API integration in a README.md using sequence diagrams to show you understand the flow of data, not just the code.
Include a 'fallback UI' for when the API is down—it shows you think about production resilience, not just the 'happy path'.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, deploy it to Vercel and write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining the architectural choices you made during this integration.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student following instructions; you are an engineer shipping software. The gap between you and the senior dev is just a few more integrations away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
